name: Orellana (Ecuador)
description: A black vertical band along the hoist (about one-fifth of the width) charged with four white five-pointed stars arranged in a vertical column; the remaining fly bears three horizontal stripes — white, green and yellow.
history: Orellana Province was created on 30 July 1998 from territory previously in Napo Province, the most recent of Ecuador's Amazonian provinces. Its capital is Puerto Francisco de Orellana (Coca); the province is named for the explorer Francisco de Orellana.

features.role: four white stars in a vertical column on the black hoist band

features.symbolism: horizontal triband on the fly side of the flag
